heading: Owners engaged primarily in manufacturing or mineral industry
label: 12118 Owners engaged primarily in manufacturing or mineral industry

The functions of the Secretary of the Treasury relating to the Coast Guard previously were transferred to the Secretary of Transportation by section 6(b) of the Department of Transportation Act (, , ). The Coast Guard and the functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating to the Coast Guard were again transferred to the Department of Homeland Security by section 888(b) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (, , ).section 2101 of title 46Pub. L. 107–296116 Stat. 2135Oct. 15, 1966Nov. 25, 2002In subsection (a)(1), the words “seeking hereunder to document a vessel under the laws of the United States or to operate a vessel exempt from documentation under the laws of the United States” are omitted as unnecessary because of the reorganization of the section.Subsection (d)(1)(B) is substituted for “together with their owners or masters, shall be entitled to all the other benefits and privileges and shall be subject to the same requirements, penalties, and forfeitures as may be applicable in the case of vessels built in the United States and otherwise documented or exempt from documentation under the laws of the United States” to eliminate unnecessary words.In subsection (d)(2), the words before subparagraph (A) are substituted for “no vessel owned by any such corporation shall engage in the fisheries or in the transportation of merchandise or passengers for hire between points in the United States, including Territories, Districts, and possessions thereof, embraced within the coastwise laws, except” to eliminate unnecessary words. See the definition of “United States” in chapter 1 of the revised title.

children.label: A
children.text: the corporation is incorporated under the laws of the United States or a State;

children.label: B
children.text: a majority of the officers and directors of the corporation are individuals who are citizens of the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: at least 90 percent of the employees of the corporation are residents of the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: the corporation is engaged primarily in a manufacturing or mineral industry in the United States;

children.label: E
children.text: the total book value of the vessels owned by the corporation is not more than 10 percent of the total book value of the assets of the corporation; and

children.label: F
children.text: the corporation buys or produces in the United States at least 75 percent of the raw materials used or sold in its operations.

children.label: A
children.text: is incorporated under the laws of the United States or a State; and

children.label: B
children.text: controls, directly or indirectly, at least 50 percent of the voting stock of a Bowaters corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: is incorporated under the laws of the United States or a State; and

children.label: B
children.text: has at least 50 percent of its voting stock controlled, directly or indirectly, by a Bowaters corporation or its parent.

children.heading: .—Deemed Citizen
children.label: b .—Deemed Citizen
children.text: A Bowaters corporation is deemed to be a citizen of the United States for purposes of chapters 121, 551, and 561 and .section 80104 of this title
is owned by a Bowaters corporation;
was built in the United States; and

children.label: A
children.text: is self-propelled and less than 500 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage as measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under ; orsection 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is not self-propelled.

children.label: A
children.text: a vessel documented under this section may engage in the coastwise trade; and

children.label: B
children.text: the vessel and its owner and master are entitled to the same benefits and are subject to the same requirements and penalties as if the vessel were otherwise documented or exempt from documentation under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: as a service for a parent or subsidiary of the corporation owning the vessel; or

children.label: i
children.text: is subject to jurisdiction under subchapter II of chapter 135 of title 49;

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise qualifies as a citizen of the United States under ; andsection 50501 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: is not owned or controlled, directly or indirectly, by the corporation owning the vessel.

children.heading: .—Validity of Corporate Certificate
children.label: e .—Validity of Corporate Certificate
children.text: A certificate filed by a corporation under this section remains valid only as long as the corporation continues to satisfy the conditions required of the corporation by this section. When a corporation no longer satisfies those conditions, the corporation loses its status under this section and immediately shall surrender to the Secretary any documents issued to it based on that status.

children.heading: .—Falsifying material fact
children.label: 1 .—Falsifying material fact
children.text: If a corporation knowingly falsifies a material fact in a certificate filed under subsection (a), the vessel (or its value) documented or operated under this section shall be forfeited.

children.heading: .—Transporting merchandise
children.label: 2 .—Transporting merchandise
children.text: If a vessel transports merchandise for hire in violation of this section, the merchandise shall be forfeited to the United States Government.

children.heading: .—Transporting passengers
children.label: 3 .—Transporting passengers
children.text: If a vessel transports passengers for hire in violation of this section, the vessel is liable for a penalty of $200 for each passenger so transported.

children.heading: .—Remission or mitigation
children.label: 4 .—Remission or mitigation
children.text: A penalty or forfeiture incurred under this subsection may be remitted or mitigated under .section 2107(b) of this title
